req.title: Hr-Admin deletes leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a leave-request through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) successful deletion by an authenticated hr-admin (EP positive path), (2) rejection when the caller is unauthenticated (NEG/401), (3) rejection when the caller has an insufficient role (NEG/403), (4) rejection when a mandatory field is missing or malformed (DT/400), (5) boundary case testing the transition of a leave-request from 'existing' to 'deleted' state and verifying the audit record is written (STC). Tests are ordered to cover at minimum one positive, one negative, and one boundary case as required.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_delete_leave_request_success
preconditions: a leave-request with a known id exists in state 'pending'
actions: Obtain a valid hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token fixture>
Create a leave-request via <create_leave_request helper> to obtain a known leave_request_id
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Audit record exists for the deletion action
trace: R-WEB-015: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_delete_leave_request_success(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
    get_audit_record,
):
    leave_request_id = create_leave_request(token=hr_admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}"

    response = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(leave_request_id)

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=leave_request_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful deletion"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_leave_request_returns_401
preconditions: a leave-request with a known id exists
actions: Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id} without an Authorization header
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_delete_leave_request_returns_401(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
    get_audit_record,
):
    leave_request_id = create_leave_request(token=hr_admin_token)
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}"

    response = requests.delete(url)

    assert response.status_code == 401

    get_resp = requests.get(
        url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Resource must still exist after failed deletion"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=leave_request_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record must be written on unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_leave_request_returns_403
preconditions: a leave-request with a known id exists
caller is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Obtain a valid employee JWT via <get_employee_token fixture>
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id} with the employee Authorization header
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_delete_leave_request_returns_403(
    hr_admin_token,
    employee_token,
    create_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
    get_audit_record,
):
    leave_request_id = create_leave_request(token=hr_admin_token)
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}

    response = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403

    get_resp = requests.get(
        url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Resource must still exist after forbidden attempt"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=leave_request_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record must be written on forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_leave_request_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: request body omits the mandatory 'reason' field required by the delete endpoint
actions: Obtain a valid hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token fixture>
Send DELETE /api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id} with an empty JSON body (omitting mandatory fields)
expected: Response body contains a validation error message
trace: R-WEB-015: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_leave_request_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
):
    leave_request_id = create_leave_request(token=hr_admin_token)
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}"
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }

    response = requests.delete(url, json={}, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 400
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body or "errors" in body

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Resource must still exist after validation failure"

tests.name: test_delete_leave_request_state_transition_and_audit
preconditions: leave-request exists in state 'pending' (boundary: last valid state before deletion)
actions: Obtain a valid hr-admin JWT via <get_hr_admin_token fixture>
Create a leave-request in state 'pending' via <create_leave_request helper>
Verify leave-request state is 'pending' via GET
Attempt a second DELETE on the same id (idempotency / already-deleted boundary)
expected: First DELETE returns 200 or 201 with entity id
Audit record exists after first DELETE
GET after first DELETE returns 404 (state: deleted)
Second DELETE returns 404 or 409 (resource no longer exists)
trace: R-WEB-015: response 200 or 201 with entity id
State boundary: pending -> deleted -> attempt re-delete
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_leave_request_state_transition_and_audit(
    hr_admin_token,
    create_leave_request,
    api_base_url,
    get_audit_record,
):
    leave_request_id = create_leave_request(token=hr_admin_token, state="pending")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}
    url = f"{api_base_url}/api/leave-requests/{leave_request_id}"

    pre_state = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert pre_state.status_code == 200
    assert pre_state.json().get("state") == "pending"

    del_resp = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert str(del_resp.json().get("id")) == str(leave_request_id)

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=leave_request_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after first deletion"

    post_state = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert post_state.status_code == 404

    second_del = requests.delete(url, headers=headers)
    assert second_del.status_code in (404, 409), (
        f"Re-delete of absent resource must return 404 or 409, got {second_del.status_code}"
    )
